commit.message:
fix: replace useSyncExternalStore with useState+useEffect for watchlist

useSyncExternalStore's getServerSnapshot was returning a new array
reference each render, causing an infinite loop (React error #185).
Reverted to useState+useEffect pattern with a stable empty array
constant to avoid hydration mismatch.

fix: Telegram messages cut off — multi-line [NOTIFY] regex, restore skills (#45)

[NOTIFY] regex used `.+` which only captures a single line. Multi-line
messages (lists, clarification questions) had everything after the first
newline silently dropped. Fixed to capture until the next tag or end of
text. Also removed redundant 3500-char hard truncation (telegram.py
already splits at 4096).

Restored three skills from the unmerged `skills` branch:
- /tune (renamed from /response-tuning)
- /draft-issue
- /standup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make effect animation speed independent of frame rate

Effects used frame-count-based animation (t += 1.0 per frame), so their
visual speed was tied to actual FPS achieved. When draw() was slow (e.g.
before a reboot), effects ran visibly slower; when fast, they sped up.

frame_sleep() now returns (next_deadline, dt) where dt is the actual
elapsed time since the previous frame. All effects scale their animation
increment by dt * fps, which equals 1.0 at target FPS but compensates
when frames run slow or fast. dt is capped at 3x interval to prevent
huge jumps after system suspend/resume.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add read-only SQL query tool for efficient MCP access (#62)

Defense-in-depth read-only SQL endpoint (POST /api/query/sql):

1. PRAGMA query_only = ON via separate read-only SQLite engine
2. Pydantic field_validator blocks writes with friendly 422 errors
3. Semicolon blocking prevents multi-statement injection
4. Whitespace normalization + word-boundary tokenization
5. Real 5s query timeout via SQLite progress_handler
6. Proper error classification (400/403/408/500)
7. 250 row limit, key-value dict response format

30 tests covering happy path, 9 injection bypass vectors, error
handling, and auth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add BBL minimum ventilation rate auto-calculation

Calculate ventilation rates automatically from BBL Afdeling 3.6 when
not explicitly specified per room. Makes ventilation_rate optional on
Room — when null, the engine uses BBL minimums based on room function
and floor area (e.g. 0.9 dm³/s per m² for living spaces, 21 dm³/s
for kitchen exhaust, 14 dm³/s for bathroom, 7 dm³/s for toilet).

Adds q_v_minimum to VentilationResult so the frontend always shows
the BBL minimum. Frontend shows BBL minimum as placeholder in the
ventilation input field.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(bot): store bot responses in raw_messages and buffer

After send_group_text(), insert the bot's response into raw_messages
with sender_hash=bot and append to the group buffer with [BOT] tag.
Signal doesn't echo sent messages back, so without this the bot's
own responses were invisible to future context/gate/agents — causing
repeated advice and broken follow-up detection.

Add BST/GMT season check — skip wrong-season cron fires

Each of the 3 capture times (10pm, midnight, 7am) has two cron
entries: one for GMT, one for BST. The workflow now checks the
current UK timezone offset and skips execution if the cron that
fired belongs to the wrong season. Exactly 3 pushes per day,
no duplicates.

commit.message:
fix: 5 HA cluster correctness issues (#185-#189)

#187: xdp_zone NO_NEIGH drops VLAN context — add vlan_iface_map lookup
before check_egress_rg_active() so VLAN subinterfaces resolve to correct
parent ifindex + vlan_id for RG active check.

#189: RGVRRPReady false positive — add hasRETH parameter so VRRP-less
RGs (e.g. RG 0 control-plane) are only considered ready when they
genuinely have no RETH requirement, not just because other RGs have
VRRP instances.

#188: RGInterfaceReady treats missing local interfaces as peer-owned —
use InterfaceSlot() + SlotToNodeID() to determine ownership. Only skip
interfaces confirmed to belong to peer; missing local interfaces now
correctly fail readiness.

#186: Fabric readiness decoupled from takeover gating — add
fabricPopulated check gated on PeerAlive() to prevent promotion before
fabric cross-chassis forwarding is usable.

#185: Zone-RG mapping limitation — remove early break, scan all
interfaces per zone, warn if multiple RGs detected (ambiguous session
sync ownership).

Add GitHub Actions workflow to publish wheels to PyPI

Builds native wheels for Linux (x86-64, aarch64), macOS (x86-64, arm64),
and Windows (x86-64) on every v* tag push, plus an sdist. Uses
maturin-action for wheel builds and pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish with
OIDC trusted publishing for the upload step.
